name: Scope Guards / defer
def: Registering cleanup actions that run automatically when a scope exits, however it exits.
problem: Guarantee resource release / undo on every exit path (return, break, exception) without duplication.
mechanism: A guard object runs a closure in its destructor (RAII) or a defer statement queues cleanup to run on scope exit; commit() can dismiss the rollback.
failures: Forgetting to dismiss a rollback guard on success
whenNot: Languages with full RAII often need no explicit guard
myths: "Only Go has defer" — C++ scope guards, C# using, Java try-with-resources are equivalents
